message_text:
Your buyers budgeted for the mortgage. They probably accounted for taxes and insurance. But did anyone mention the other $21,400?

That's the average annual cost of owning a single-family home beyond the mortgage — covering utilities, maintenance, insurance, and more — according to Bankrate's 2025 Hidden Costs of Homeownership Study. In high-cost states, it can reach $29,000. And in Bankrate's companion survey, 42% of homeowners said hidden maintenance costs were their single biggest regret about buying. Not the neighborhood. Not the floor plan. The bills.

Two homes at the same price and square footage can have wildly different operating costs depending on the age, condition, and efficiency of their systems. HVAC, water heating, insulation, appliances — these are the line items that drive monthly costs, and they're rarely part of the listing conversation.

Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar gives agents and buyers a way to compare homes on what they'll actually cost to run — not just what they cost to buy. It surfaces system efficiency, estimated operating costs, and how a home compares to similar properties early in the search, when it can inform decisions rather than create regrets.

Read more → pearlscore.com/news/what-every-buyer-needs-to-know-about-operating-costs

Average annual homeownership costs beyond the mortgage: $21,400 (Bankrate). 42% of owners say hidden costs were their biggest regret. Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar shows what a home will cost to run — early.

You budgeted for the mortgage. You accounted for taxes and insurance. But the average single-family homeowner spends an additional $21,400 per year on utilities, maintenance, and other operating costs, according to Bankrate's 2025 study. In high-cost states, it can hit $29,000.

It's not just the amount — it's the surprise. 42% of homeowners told Bankrate that hidden maintenance costs were their single biggest regret about buying.

The thing is, much of this is predictable if you know where to look. The age and efficiency of the HVAC system, the condition of the water heater, insulation levels, appliance efficiency — these are the drivers. They're just not always visible in a listing.

Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar makes them visible. It estimates what a home will cost to run and how it compares to similar homes nearby — so whether you're buying or evaluating your own home, you can plan with real numbers instead of hoping for the best.

Look up any home at pearlscore.com.

Two homes. Same price. Same neighborhood. Same square footage. But one costs thousands more per year to run.

The difference is in the systems you don't see in listing photos — HVAC age and efficiency, insulation quality, water heater condition, appliance performance. Bankrate's 2025 study found the average homeowner spends $21,400/year beyond the mortgage on these costs. But that's an average. The range between an efficient home and an inefficient one can be significant — and it compounds every single month.

This is exactly the kind of information that helps agents have better conversations with buyers. Not just "what does this home cost to buy?" but "what will it cost to own?" Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar makes that comparison possible early — estimating operating costs and system efficiency so buyers can evaluate homes on total cost of ownership.

The homes that run efficiently have a story worth telling. And the agents who can tell it are having a different kind of conversation with their clients.

Two homes at $400,000. Same square footage. Same neighborhood. But one costs thousands more per year to run because of an aging HVAC system, minimal insulation, and an inefficient water heater. Your buyers won't see that difference in a listing.

Bankrate puts average annual costs beyond the mortgage at $21,400 — but the gap between an efficient home and an inefficient one can be dramatic. Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar makes that gap visible early, so agents can help buyers compare total cost of ownership, not just sticker price.

Two homes at the same price can cost very different amounts to actually live in. The difference comes down to things you won't see in listing photos — the age and efficiency of the HVAC system, insulation quality, water heater condition, and appliance performance.

Bankrate's 2025 study found the average homeowner spends $21,400/year beyond the mortgage. But that's an average — and the gap between an efficient home and an inefficient one can add up to thousands of dollars every year.

The good news? These cost drivers are measurable. And homes that run efficiently have a real advantage that's been hard to see until now. Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar estimates what a home will cost to run and compares it to similar properties nearby — so you can evaluate total cost of ownership before you make an offer.

Look up any home at pearlscore.com.
